name: Metaconceptual Art Knowledge Graph
description: Canonical, machine-readable knowledge graph for Metaconceptual Art. Each node carries a stable site ID and, where it corresponds to a real concept, person, or movement, a Wikidata QID plus the art-domain authority IDs (Getty ULAN for people, Getty AAT for concepts, Grove Art Online) so the museum connects outward to both the general and the art-specific linked-data graph. Artwork nodes additionally point to a per-object Linked Art (CIDOC-CRM) JSON-LD representation.
groveArtNote: Grove Art Online IDs (Oxford) are recorded for reference; the resource is subscription-gated, so the site links to the open Getty vocab URIs.

prefixes.source: Person, text, or origin cited by the work
prefixes.role: A position activated within the system

nodes.label: The Website Itself
nodes.description: The public page treated as the artwork: a surface where language, navigation, metadata, revision, and viewing conditions become material.

nodes.label: Eight Sentences On Metaconceptual Art
nodes.description: A sentence sequence after Sol LeWitt framing Metaconceptual Art as a field of form, institution, market, history, medium, access, and life.

nodes.label: Construction of the Museum as Concept
nodes.description: The museum appears inside the artwork as something still being built: institutional authority shown as architecture, ornament, labor, and frame.

nodes.label: Conceptual art
nodes.description: Contemporary art movement that shifts attention from the handmade object to the idea.

nodes.label: Institutional critique
nodes.description: Artistic practice that treats the museum, market, gallery, and frame as the material of the work.

nodes.label: Systems art
nodes.description: Art influenced by cybernetics and systems theory, foregrounding relation, process, and feedback over the discrete object.

nodes.label: Internet art
nodes.description: Art that uses the website, protocol, page, and browser as its artistic site.

nodes.label: Found object / readymade
nodes.description: Art created from undisguised, often modified, objects designated as art by context rather than craft.

nodes.label: Provenance
nodes.description: The traceable chronology of ownership, custody, and lineage of an object, idea, or claim.

nodes.label: Knowledge graph
nodes.description: An information repository structured as a graph of entities and relationships, the form the meta-museum takes.

nodes.label: Sol LeWitt
nodes.description: American artist (1928-2007); his Sentences on Conceptual Art establish the sentence as a conceptual, rule-based unit.

nodes.label: Joseph Kosuth
nodes.description: American conceptual artist (born 1945); art as inquiry into the conditions of art.

nodes.label: Marcel Duchamp
nodes.description: French artist (1887-1968); designation, context, and the readymade.

nodes.label: Provenance Layer
nodes.description: Tracks authorship, revision, and the lineage of every claim and work.

nodes.label: Viewer
nodes.description: The position that activates meaning by reading, querying, and interpreting the work.

nodes.label: Wikidata Layer
nodes.description: Grounded the semantic layer in Wikidata identifiers and added a live related-concepts query.
